item.description:
## What's New in CueNow 1.3.0
**Delete notes right from the menu bar** Hover over any note in the menu bar list and click the trash icon to delete it — no need to open the note first. Especially handy for notes you've made fully transparent.
**Launch at startup** Enable "Launch at startup" in Settings and CueNow opens automatically when you log in to your Mac, reopening your sticky notes right where you left them.
**Error logging** A new optional setting records what the app is doing so that, if something goes wrong, you can share a log to help us fix it faster. It's off by default, and your note contents are never included.
**Transparent notes stay findable** Notes set to 0% opacity now stay just barely visible, so you can always find and click them.
**More polish** - The active-note dot in the menu now matches your Mac's accent color. - The Settings window now tucks away neatly with Stage Manager, like a normal app window. - Smaller, cleaner settings toggles.

- Fixed an issue where typed text could be invisible in notes on some Macs. - Added a translucent (glass) note style — choose Solid or Glass in Settings. - Redesigned the App Updates tab and made release notes easier to read. - CueNow now checks for updates automatically and shows what's new. - Added numbered lists; type "1." or "1)" to start one. - Create checklists by typing "- " (hyphen and space). - Improved checklist editing: cleaner deletes and automatic renumbering. - Apply bold, italic, or underline before you start typing. - Added an option to hide CueNow's Dock icon and run from the menu bar.
- Note History now shows more archived notes at once and scrolls on its own. - You can now hide CueNow's Dock icon and run it from the menu bar.
